     The Numbers Business Report

Little Black Book (2004)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $20,422,207Details
International Box Office $1,336,164
Worldwide Box Office $21,758,371
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Stacey is an associate producer for a daytime talk show featuring Kippie Kann. She’s upset that her boyfriend, Derek, won't talk about his past relationships. With encouragement from her co-worker Barb, Stacey steals Derek’s phone, contacts his ex-girlfriends and interviews them under the pretense that they’ll be on the show. But things get complicated when Stacey forms a friendship with one of the girls.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$7,075,217 (34.6% of total gross)
Legs:2.89 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:93.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$30,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,445 opening theaters/2,445 max. theaters, 3.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $39,331,656

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 6th, 2004 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
Video Release: January 4th, 2005 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sexual content/humor and language
Running Time: 106 minutes
Plot point: Relationship Advice, Romance
Social setting: TV Industry
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Romantic Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Revolution Studios, Blue Star Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Stacey is an associate producer for a daytime talk show featuring Kippie Kann. She’s upset that her boyfriend, Derek, won't talk about his past relationships. With encouragement from her co-worker Barb, Stacey steals Derek’s phone, contacts his ex-girlfriends and interviews them under the pretense that they’ll be on the show. But things get complicated when Stacey forms a friendship with one of the girls.
